team.purpose: To manage, troubleshoot and innovate IT infrastructure

team.name: cybersecurity-team
team.header: Team responsible for Elexion Automotive's cybersecurity
team.purpose: To ensure the security of company data and systems

channel.name: cybersecurity_trends
channel.header: Discussion about external cybersecurity market trends
channel.purpose: To stay updated about the latest cybersecurity trends

channel.name: threat_intelligence
channel.header: Discussion about threats to Elexion Automotive's systems
channel.purpose: To analyze and react to emerging threats

post.message: Good morning all, remember we have a meeting scheduled today at 3 pm to discuss the budget allocation for the next quarter. Please make sure to go through the details before the meeting.
FYI, I've just completed the analysis of the recent phishing attempts and will be sharing the report in the afternoon meeting. Let's discuss preventive measures then.
Thanks for the heads up, Jane. I've been reviewing the proposed budgets and have some suggestions regarding the technology upgrades planned for next quarter.
John, can we also discuss the new incident response protocol during the meeting? I believe it needs some fine-tuning based on the recent incidents.
Absolutely, Ethan. Your inputs are valuable. Let's discuss your suggestions in the meeting as well.

Team, I've been thinking about the information from the April 2025 article from Help Net Security. It's clear that we need to take a deeper dive into the external cybersecurity market data relevant to the automotive industry. As you all know, we currently dedicate just over half of our time on threat intelligence, which is crucial for our consumer data protection and safety measures.

You're right, Ethan. Protecting consumer data and ensuring safety are our top priorities. It's only logical that we dedicate a significant portion of our time to stay ahead of the curve. How do you propose we manage our resources?

I propose we incrementally increase the portion of our time dedicated to analyzing external cybersecurity market data. By the end of Q4 2025, I believe we should aim for roughly 60% of our effort being dedicated to this. This shift will help ensure that we're utilizing the most relevant and current information to bolster our defenses and protect our customers.
